chunks.text: Clark Nguyen is a Professor at UC Berkeley's Department of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences. He received his B.S., M.S., and Ph.D. from UC Berkeley in Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences in 1988, 1991, and 1994 respectively. He previously served as a professor at the University of Michigan from 1995 to 2006. Currently, he is a co-Director of the Berkeley Sensor & Actuator Center. His research focuses on micro electromechanical systems (MEMS), including integrated micromechanical signal processors, sensors, RF communication architectures, and integrated circuit design.
chunks.question: What is Clark Nguyen's current position and research focus?
chunks.answer: Professor at UC Berkeley focusing on MEMS.

chunks.text: Prof. Nguyen has held significant roles in industry and government. He founded Discera, Inc. in 2001, commercializing MEMS-based timing products, and served as its Vice President and CTO until 2002. From 2002 to 2005, he was a Program Manager at DARPA, overseeing multiple programs such as MEMS, Chip-Scale Atomic Clock, and RF MEMS Improvement. He also contributed to NASA's New Millennium Integrated Product Development Team from 1995 to 1997, roadmapping future communications technologies.
chunks.question: What key industry and government roles has Clark Nguyen held?
chunks.answer: Founded Discera and was a DARPA program manager.

chunks.text: Prof. Nguyen has received numerous awards, including the 1938E Award for Research and Teaching Excellence from the University of Michigan in 1998, the Ruth and Joel Spira Award for Outstanding Teaching in 2000, and the Henry Russel Award in 2001. He earned the IEEE UFFC Cady Award in 2006 and the IEEE Robert Bosch Micro and Nano Electro Mechanical Systems Award in 2017. Additionally, he has won multiple best paper awards, such as the 2007 IEEE Transactions on Ultrasonics, Ferroelectrics, and Frequency Control Best Paper Award.
chunks.question: What are some notable awards Clark Nguyen has received?
chunks.answer: IEEE Robert Bosch MEMS Award and multiple teaching awards.

chunks.text: He has organized and chaired 37 IEEE and DARPA workshops, serving as Technical Program Chair and General Chair for the 2010 and 2011 IEEE International Frequency Control Symposiums. Prof. Nguyen was a Co-General Chair of the 2017 IEEE International Micro Electro Mechanical Systems Conference. He is a Fellow of the IEEE and served as President of the IEEE Ultrasonics, Ferroelectrics, and Frequency Control Society from 2016 to 2017. Currently, he is the Senior Past-President of the society and founded the IEEE MEMS Technical Community in 2018.
chunks.question: What leadership roles has Clark Nguyen held in professional societies?
chunks.answer: President of IEEE UFFC Society and founded IEEE MEMS community.

chunks.text: His research areas include Micro/Nano Electro Mechanical Systems (MEMS), Integrated Circuits, Physical Electronics, and Design, Modeling and Analysis. He is affiliated with the Berkeley Sensor & Actuator Center. Selected publications cover topics like low-power MEMS-based oscillators, RF channel-select micromechanical disk filters, high-Q resonators, and capacitive-piezoelectric transducers, demonstrating his contributions to MEMS technology and frequency control.
chunks.question: What are Clark Nguyen's main research areas and affiliations?
chunks.answer: MEMS, integrated circuits, and Berkeley Sensor & Actuator Center.

chunks.text: Selected honors and awards include the IEEE EDS Robert Bosch Micro and Nano Electro Mechanical Systems Award in 2017, the Electrical Engineering Award for Outstanding Teaching in 2013, and being named an IEEE Fellow in 2007. He also received the SSCS ISSCC Jack Raper Award for Outstanding Technology Directions Paper in 2006 and the UFFC W. G. Cady Frequency Control Award in 2006, highlighting his impact in both research and education.
chunks.question: What recent honors highlight Clark Nguyen's achievements?
chunks.answer: IEEE Bosch MEMS Award and Outstanding Teaching Award.
